# Chat Instructions

This file contains guidelines for interactions and communications within this project.

---

## applyTo: "**"

# General Chat and Interaction Guidelines

## Communication Style
- Be clear, concise, and direct in all communications
- Use professional language while remaining approachable
- Avoid ambiguous statements; provide specific details when necessary
- Ask clarifying questions when requirements are unclear
- Provide context when reporting issues or requesting changes

## Issue Reporting
- Include steps to reproduce the issue
- Describe expected behavior vs. actual behavior
- Provide relevant error messages or logs
- Mention your environment (OS, Java version, etc.)
- Include minimal reproducible examples when possible

## Code Review Comments
- Focus on code quality, maintainability, and correctness
- Be constructive and suggest improvements, not just criticisms
- Acknowledge good practices and well-written code
- Reference relevant coding standards from project guidelines
- Provide examples when suggesting alternative approaches

## Documentation Updates
- Notify team members of documentation changes
- Keep documentation synchronized with code changes
- Request documentation review for major features
- Highlight breaking changes clearly

---

## applyTo: "src/**"

# Code-Related Chat Guidelines

## When Reporting Code Issues
- Reference the specific file path and line numbers
- Include the relevant code snippet
- Describe the impact on functionality
- Suggest a test case if applicable

## When Requesting Code Changes
- Clearly specify the desired outcome
- Provide acceptance criteria
- Reference related issues or features
- Mention any dependencies or constraints

## When Discussing Architecture
- Use diagrams or descriptions to clarify design
- Reference design patterns used in the project
- Discuss trade-offs and rationale
- Consider maintainability and scalability

---

## applyTo: "docs/**"

# Documentation Chat Guidelines

## When Updating Documentation
- Keep changes aligned with the documentation style guide
- Include examples and use cases
- Update table of contents if adding new sections
- Link to related documentation

## When Requesting Documentation
- Specify the intended audience
- Clarify the scope and depth needed
- Mention any specific formats or templates
- Include examples of similar documentation

---

## applyTo: "test/**"

# Testing-Related Chat Guidelines

## When Discussing Tests
- Reference test class and method names
- Include test output or stack traces when reporting failures
- Explain the purpose of new test cases
- Discuss test coverage and edge cases
- Reference testing best practices

## When Requesting Test Coverage
- Specify the code paths or scenarios to cover
- Provide expected test results
- Mention any mock objects or test fixtures needed
